characters.description: 514AAliasSubject 514AFiveBruce WayneBruce 2AffiliationThe Court of OwlsStatusUnknownFirst"Wrath of the Villains: Transference"Last"Heroes Rise: All Will Be Judged"Appeared in9 episodes(see below)Portrayed byDavid MazouzSource 514A, also known asBruce Wayne, is a clone of the originalBruce Waynethat was created inIndian Hill. He had escaped intoGotham City, along withHugo Strange's other experiments due toFish Mooney. 514A was created by ProfessorHugo Strangein the research facilityIndian Hill, located underneathArkham Asylum. He was made as an exact clone ofBruce Wayne.

characters.name: Adams
characters.description: AdamsAffiliationRichard SionisStatusAliveFirst"The Mask"Portrayed byBrian MorvantSource Adamsis an employee ofSionis Investments. Adams told detectivesJim GordonandHarvey BullockthatSionisran a circuit where he forced all potential employees to fight to the death for a job. Before Jim and Bullock could get an official confession from Adams, Sionis sent a lawyer to represent Adams.[1]

characters.name: Agnes
characters.description: AgnesAliasSkinnerFamilyUnnamed (Grandchildren)AffiliationFalcone Crime Family(Formerly)OccupationAssassin (Formerly)StatusAliveFirst"A Dark Knight: The Sinking Ship, The Grand Applause"Appeared in1Portrayed byWaltrudis BuckSourceAgnes, also known as theScandinavianSkinner, is a former assassin that previously worked forCarmine Falcone. As the Scandinavian Skinner, Agnes was one ofCarmine Falcone's deadliest assassins. However, at some point, she ended up spending some time in prison thanks toHarvey Bullock. After being released, Agnes retired and opened up a candy shop. She kept her criminal past a secret from her grandchildren.

characters.description: AgnesAliasSkinnerFamilyUnnamed (Grandchildren)AffiliationFalcone Crime Family(Formerly)OccupationAssassin (Formerly)StatusAliveFirst"A Dark Knight: The Sinking Ship, The Grand Applause"Appeared in1Portrayed byWaltrudis BuckSourceAgnes, also known as theScandinavianSkinner, is a former assassin that previously worked forCarmine Falcone. characters.name: Nyssa al Ghul
characters.description: Nyssa al GhulFull nameNyssa al GhulAliasTheresa WalkerFamilyRa's al Ghul(father; deceased)AffiliationU.S. Government (formerly)League of ShadowsDelta Force(subordinates, formerly)Bane(subordinate, formerly)Hugo Strange(associate, formerly)OccupationSecretary of Homeland Security (formerly, as Walker)StatusAliveFirstLegend of the Dark Knight: Year ZeroAppeared in5 episodes(see below)Portrayed byJaime MurraySource Hugo Strange(associate, formerly) Nyssa al Ghul, aliasTheresa Walker, is the former Secretary of Homeland Security and the daughter ofRa's al Ghul, the legendary head of theLeague of Shadows. When Gotham becameNo Man’s Land, Nyssa went rogue and plotted to have all of the criminals in the city illegally killed. She also sabotaged any efforts made to help the citizens, resulting in the deaths of innocent civilians. After her activities were exposed, Nyssa went into Gotham to make sure her plans succeeded. At first, Nyssa seemed kind and sympathetic to Jim’s plight. However, she is really cold and calculating. She used Edward Nygma like a tool to destroy Haven, killing 300 people and shooting down a Wayne Enterprises relief chopper. These are acts that Nygma and many other criminals, despite having previously committed murder themselves, found deplorable. At the same time, Edward was a disposable pawn to her and she wanted Jim to kill him once he had outlived his usefulness to her. She is consumed with revenge and utterly spiteful (possibly petty as well), as her agenda to destroy Gotham is not merely to carry on her father's work but also take revenge upon Bruce Wayne for killing her father. Her thirst for revenge extends toBarbara Keanfor her equal complicity in the killing of Ra's. She seems to be a little cold-hearted when she orders Eduardo to hunt down Barbara, knowing that she is pregnant. She intends to make Bruce suffer by forcing him to watch as everything and everyone he cares about is destroyed before she kills him. Also, Nyssa shows some type of selfishness when she decided to leave Gotham without telling her subordinate,Bane, or the otherDelta Forcesoldiers, proving she was just looking out for herself. Ultimately, she is much more a monster than her father; Nyssa has no qualms with murdering and harming others in unnecessarily drawn-out plans.

characters.name: Ra's al Ghul
characters.description: Ra's al GhulFull nameRa's al GhulFamilyNyssa al Ghul(daughter)AffiliationLeague of ShadowsOccupationFounder and Leader of the League of Shadows (formerly)Founder of The Sirens (formerly)StatusDeceased(Stabbed with Embalming Knife of King Balahsi)First"Heroes Rise: Destiny Calling"Appeared in13 episodes(see below)Portrayed byAlexander SiddigSource Founder of The Sirens (formerly) Ra's al Ghul(roughly translated to "Head of the Ghoul/Demon") was a mysterious individual, the supreme leader of the assassin organizationLeague of Shadowsand the father ofNyssa al Ghul. Being alive for thousands of years, he has gained eternal life through usage of restorativeLazarus Pits. He also foresawBruce Wayne's destiny to be the protector of the newGotham Cityand turning him into his heir. He initially tried to turn Barbara Kean into his successor as the Demon's Head, but eventually took that power back from her when he deemed her "unworthy" for not following the League's traditional goals. Ra's was able to finally make his vision of Gotham's destruction come true when he allied withJeremiah Valeska. Ra's al Ghul was extremely ruthless and was a dedicated leader of the league of assassins. He was shown to have a respectful and honourable side of him, as shown withBruce Wayne, as he respects him for who he is and has a vision of him being "Gotham's Dark Knight". This side was also shown when he resurrectedBarbara Keanfrom theLazarus Pit, as he understood the pain and suffering Barbara had been through. Despite his respect for the two, he was willing to kidnap Bruce and attempt to kill Barbara just to get who he wants. He held a knife byTabitha Galavan's neck if Barbara didn't give him back the Demon's head. He killedAlex Winthropby slitting his throat and brainwashedBruce Wayneto murderAlfred Pennyworth. This shows how psychopathic and delusional he truly is. Ra's al Ghul only sees beneath his own vision and doesn't seemed to care about how many lives are at stake. As he teamed up withJeremiah Valeskato destroy the bridges regardless of how many lives would be taken.

characters.name: Alex
characters.description: AlexFull nameUnknownOccupationPharmacistStatusAliveFirst"Mad City: Better to Reign in Hell..."Appeared in1 episode(see below)Portrayed byKenny WongSourceAlexis a pharmacist. When theRidgeback Monsterattempts to rob the pharmacy, he attacks Alex, throwing him through the window and onto the streets. When the monster makes a go for him,Jim Gordonarrives on the scene looking to subdue the monster and bring him in as part of a bounty. The pair get into a fight, with the imposing and strong monster gaining the upper hand. The fight ends when the monster makes a go for Gordon, only to carelessly walk out onto the road and get struck down by a truck.[1]

characters.name: Allusions to other Batman media
characters.description: Below is a list of characters, locations, etc. who originally appear in the otherBatman Mediaprior to the episode's airing, by appearance.

characters.name: Allusions to the DC Universe
characters.description: For allusions to other adaptations of Batman comics, seeAllusions to other Batman media Below is a list of characters, locations, etc. who originally appear in theDC comicsmultiverse prior to the episode's airing, by appearance.

characters.name: Carlos Alvarez
characters.description: Carlos AlvarezFull nameCarlos AlvarezFamilyUnnamed(wife)AffiliationGotham City Police DepartmentOccupationDetectiveStatusAliveFirst"Arkham"Appeared in32 episodes(see below)Portrayed byJ.W. CortesSource Carlos Alvarezis a detective who works in theGotham City Police Department. Whilst dismissive of new recruitJim Gordonin the beginning, he later became one of his most trusted allies in the fight against crime inGotham Cityin the years to come. Alvarez was one of the many cops to leave the GCPD building whenVictor Zsaszcame looking for DetectiveJames Gordon.[1]When Gordon arrested a doctor with unethical practices that Alvarez regularly used as a source, Alvarez argued with him. He later was reluctant to assist DetectiveBullockwith searching buildings that were used to hostRichard Sionis's fights, though he decided to help as long as Bullock agreed to keep covering with his wife.[2]

Anatoly andMarcostop at an incinerator to dispose ofButch Gilzean. Unknowingly, Butch escaped and killed both Anatoly and Marco.[1]

characters.name: Anderson
characters.description: AndersonOccupationDoctorStatusAliveFirst"The Scarecrow"Portrayed byPeter Jay FernandezSource Andersonis a doctor inGotham City. Anderson talked to DetectiveJim GordonaboutJonathan Crane's condition, after being injected with condensed hormones by his father,Gerald. After having run CAT scan to determine whether or not he had suffered any brain damage, and discovered while the hormones themselves had worn off, the effects had lingered, leaving Jonathan in a constant state of fear, seeing the thing he feared the most non-stop.[1]

characters.name: Anita
characters.description: AnitaOccupationMaidStatusDeceasedFirst"Spirit of the Goat"Portrayed byDenia BracheSource Anitawas the maid for the Copley Family. Anita was assistingEmber Copleyin packing for her trip on her family's boat, the Marina. After being asked to get her snacks, Anita was murdered by theSpirit of the Goatwhile returning with the snacks.[1]

characters.name: Anton
characters.description: AntonStatusDeceasedFirst"Mad City: The Executioner"Portrayed byJeremy CrutchleySource Antonwas a wealthy man who had an attraction towardsIvy Pepper. He also had a piece of jewelry that contained a key for a safe owned byThe Court of Owls. Anton bragged about his wealth and told Ivy about a safe that contained his most valuable items. Ivy told him to smell her perfume, which caused Anton to obey her command. Ivy commanded him to open his safe. She looted his safe and ran off with a necklace. WhenBruce,Selinaand Ivy went back to Anton's home to return the necklace, they found Anton dead with an arrow through his eye.[1]

characters.name: Anton

characters.name: Anubis
characters.description: AnubisAffiliationLeague of ShadowsOccupationTrackerStatusDeceasedFirst"A Dark Knight: The Demon's Head"Appeared in1 episodes(see below)Portrayed byAnthony RodriguezSourceAnubiswas a feral, fanged dog-man who was a member of theLeague of Shadows. He traveled toGotham Cityto hunt downAlex WinthropforRa's al Ghul. Anubis is a member of theLeague of Shadowswho alongside his handlerThe Hunterwas enlisted byRa's al Ghulto track downAlex Winthropin order to get theMesopotamian embalming knifethat his grandfatherNiles Winthropwas tracking. WhenBruce WayneandJim Gordontracked Alex to theGotham City Public Library, the three of them were attacked by Anubis and The Hunter. While Gordon fought The Hunter, Bruce and Alex ran from Anubis where they managed to get away. When at theGotham Natural History Museum, Anubis and the Hunter tracked down Bruce and Alex just as Gordon arrived. Using a bone from one of the skeletons, Gordon threw it out the window with Anubis following it.[1]

characters.name: Arbogast
characters.description: ArbogastAffiliationGotham City Police DepartmentOccupationOfficerStatusAliveFirst"Selina Kyle"Portrayed byJoe StarrSource Arbogast, also known asBo, is a member of theGotham City Police Department. Arbogast metJames GordonandHarvey Bullockat the scene of adead war veteran. James berates him for not being there first despite Bo being the first to respond. Arbogast explains he was looking into a case for the restaurant that pays him fifty dollars a month to "look out for them" rather than a wino nobody cares about, a statement which almost provokes Gordon into hitting him.[1]

characters.description: ArmandAffiliationCobblepot crime familyStatusDeceasedFirst"Heroes Rise: These Delicate and Dark Obsessions"Portrayed byZoran KorachSource Armandwas a former member of theCobblepot crime family. AfterGabebetrayedPenguin, Armand arrived with several other armed guards. While Gabe and the others are distracted,Ivyseduces Armand by making him smell her perfume, compelling him to kill everyone but Gabe and free them. After being freed, Penguin shot Armand in the head.[1]

characters.name: Artemis
characters.description: ArtemisStatusAliveSourceArtemisis a horse trained and ridden bySofia Falcone. WhenJim Gordonarrived at theFalcone EstateinMiamito get help fromCarmine Falcone, he witnessed Sofia dismounting from Artemis and the two watched each other curiously.[1] A framed photograph of Sofia riding Artemis in Miami could be seen at theFalcone Mansionon multiple occasions.[2][3]

characters.name: Asian Goon
characters.description: Asian GoonAffiliationCobblepot crime familyOccupationMobsterStatusAliveFirstThe Blind Fortune TellerPortrayed byUncredited ExtraSource ThisAsian Goonwas a henchman ofOswald Cobblepot, and a member ofhis gang. He is seen playing cards withgabeandanother goonatOswald's Nightclub.

At present dayHugo Strangeused Azrael's mythical story to brainwash a resurrected and enhancedTheo Galavan, making him believe he was the warrior.

characters.name: Ginny Babcock
characters.description: Ginny BabcockOccupationinternal Affairs OfficerStatusAliveFirst"Wrath of the Villains: Into the Woods"Portrayed byCathy TrienSource Ginny Babcockis an internal affairs officer in theGCPD. Ginny andHarvey Bullockmake out. Ginny goes into the bedroom, while Harvey steals her keys and gives them toJim Gordon, who was waiting outside.[1]

characters.name: Gloria Bainbridge
characters.description: Gloria BainbridgeOccupationMother of the YearStatusDeceasedFirst"A Dark Knight: That's Entertainment"Portrayed byBrenny RabineSource Gloria Bainbridgewas one of the significant high-ranked officials inGotham City. At night, Interim MayorHolden Pritchard, CommissionerReynolds, ArchbishopMcGregor,Gertrude Haverstock, and Gloria Bainbridge met at theGotham City Hallto discuss the recent events involving theLegion of Horribles. However,Jerome Valeskaand his allies went to the city hall whereMr. FreezeandMad Hattercaptured the mayor and the other members of Gotham's high society present. They took them to a van standing outside on the street, in which the other villains were waiting. Pritchard was still trying to convince Victor and Tetch to let them go, promising that he will double whatever they expect as ransom. Right then, the mayor saw Jerome Valeska standing in the van, who was scratched his nose unimpressed by these events and then smiled at the horrified mayor while Jervis handcuffed Holden to a metal rod. Upon noticing Valeska, Pritchard revealed that he has access to the police pension fund. Seemingly pensive, Jerome turned around and said "Wow, that could be a lot of money. Tempting, but, uh no, thanks.". Thereon, the mayor turned toFireflywho was standing next toPenguina few steps away from Valeska. Pritchard said "Bridgit, isn't it? I can get you safe passage from the city, money, a new home...", but Firefly smiled and responded that it was people like him that locked her up inIndian Hill, and thanks to that, there is no Bridgit anymore. Pritchard immediately turned to Tetch and called him "son", promising that he can get him a pardon and that all will be forgiven, he just needs to stop this "lunatic". However, Hatter said that "lunatic" is a little harsh, he prefers "visionary".

characters.name: Baker
characters.description: BakerOccupationJudgeStatusAliveSource Bakeris a high-ranking official of the court system inGotham City. DetectiveJim Gordoncalled Baker concerning three pharmaceutical suppliers him and his partner,Harvey Bullockwere looking to investigate.[1]

characters.name: Bam Bam
characters.description: Bam BamOccupationJudgeStatusAliveSource Judge Bam Bamis a judge inGotham City. Jim Gordon requested warrants from Judge Bam Bam to search three different pharmaceutical companies selling a drug thatDollmaker's minionsPattiandDougwere using to abduct street children.[1]

characters.name: Bannion
characters.description: BannionAffiliationFalcone crime familyOccupationIrish Mob bossStatusDeceasedFirst"Lovecraft"Portrayed byUncredited ExtraSource Bannionwas the leader of theIrish Mob, and a capo of theFalcone crime family. He was really close toCarmine Falcone, as the two came up in the underworld together. Bannion was killed by DonFalconeat a dinner meeting of the Falcone crime family, as he was supposed to guard theGotham Armory, where Falcone stored his money, though he failed at doing so.[1]It is unclear why he failed in his duty, but is believed he was either bribed by Fish Mooney or if Fish had found some way to keep him occupied while the robbery went down.

characters.name: Bannion

characters.name: Odgen Barker
characters.description: Odgen BarkerAffiliationCarmine FalconeOccupationGangsterNightclub OwnerStatusDeceasedFirst"Rise of the Villains: Damned If You Do..."Portrayed byOtto SanchezSourceOdgen Barkerwas a gangster, nightclub owner and business partner ofCarmine Falcone. Owing Falcone over $70,000, Odgen Barker refused to pay the money toPenguinwhen DonCarmine Falconeretired, as he felt his debt was forfeited. As such,Jim Gordonwas sent by Penguin to collect the debt, though Barker insisted even more that he wasn't paying Penguin, as he'd only pay to Falcone. After the two argued over the debt, Barker ended the conversation by telling Gordon to walk away, eventually pulling a gun on Jim after the latter demanded that Barker handed over the large sum of money he had on his desk. Barker was told he had to the count of three, then he was disarmed by Gordon and punched in the nose, with Gordon running out of the club with Barker's money. Barker pursued Gordon, catching him in a parking garage, and shooting at him. Gordon returned fire, and Barker was killed by him.[1]

characters.name: Barkley
characters.description: BarkleyOccupationPolice OfficerStatusAliveFirst"Wrath of the Villains: Into the Woods"Portrayed byWarren BubSource Barkleyis an officer for theGCPD. AfterJim Gordonstops a group of muggers and saves a woman, he is apprehended by Barkley. Barkley recognizes Jim and attempts to get backup, but before he is able to, he is knocked down by Jim, who claims that he's innocent.[1]
